title: carex divulsa subsp. coriogyne
intro: Carex divulsa subsp. coriogyne, also known as Coriogyne sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its distinctive leaf and stem morphology. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its chemical composition of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Coriogyne sedge has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Coriogyne sedge include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from minor ailments to chronic conditions.
